exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I bump into an old friend at the supermarket.
I don't want to bump into a car when driving in a crowded city.
She bump into the table and her leg hurt a little.

translation.en: I don't want to touch you.

example.vi: Tôi đã đụng vào cái bàn.

definitions.en: To touch or collide with something.
definitions.vi: Chạm hoặc va chạm với một cái gì đó.

notes:
đụng = to bump into or hit something, usually by accident. It can also mean to touch something with contact.

You use it with objects or people: đụng người, đụng xe, đụng tường. It often implies accidental contact, not intentional.

It can also mean “to touch” in the sense of handling something, especially in warnings. With abstract things, it can mean “to mess with” or “get involved in.”

Close synonym: va (vào) = collide with (a bit more physical/impact-focused).
exampleSentences: Sáng nay em đụng một người bạn cũ ở siêu thị.
Anh không muốn đụng xe khi lái trong thành phố đông đúc.
Chị đã đụng cái bàn và bị đau chân một chút.

translation.en: I don't want to touch you.

notes_md:
**đụng** = to bump into or hit something, usually by accident. It can also mean to touch something with contact.

You use it with objects or people: đụng người, đụng xe, đụng tường. It often implies accidental contact, not intentional.

It can also mean “to touch” in the sense of handling something, especially in warnings. With abstract things, it can mean “to mess with” or “get involved in.”


Close synonym: **va (vào)** = collide with (a bit more physical/impact-focused).
